event: History of mucosal resection for early gastric cancer
History of partial resection for left lung cancer
Did not have any heart disease
Glasgow Coma Scale of 14 (E3, V5, M6)
Crusted skin lesions on medial surface of left thigh
Freely movable adenopathy approx 2 cm in size without tenderness
Aggregated papules in right inguinal region
Activated partial thromboplastin time prolonged to 48.2 seconds
Fewer mature myeloid series cells in bone marrow
More monocytoid and lymphoid cells in bone marrow
High number of CD3-positive T cells
High number of CD4-positive T cells
Transthoracic echocardiography showed severe left ventricular dysfunction (LVEF 15%)
Electrocardiography showed slight ST elevation in V1-2 leads
Contrast-enhanced CT of chest and abdomen normal
Did not perform coronary angiography or endomyocardial biopsy due to unknown infection and bleeding tendency
Suspected severe fever with thrombocytopenia syndrome (SFTS)
Submitted serum, urine, throat swabs to public health center
Bone marrow stained positively with anti-SFTSV-nucleoprotein antibody
Considered steroid pulse therapy but did not proceed
Electrocardiography showed improvement of ST elevation
Re-submitted serum and confirmed negativity for viremia
Performed coronary angiography and endomyocardial biopsy
Coronary angiography showed no coronary artery stenosis
Endomyocardial biopsy showed myocardium mildly hypertrophic with mild myofibrillar loss and disarrangement
Immunohistochemistry revealed increases in CD3-positive T cells
Immunohistochemistry revealed increases in CD68-positive macrophages
Could not identify any infected cells with anti-SFTSV antibody staining in myocardium
Follow-up echocardiography showed ejection fraction improved to 52.5%
